title: Synergy Diligence Snoop
body: A diligence team starts asking why your merger math reads like fiction with footnotes.

title: Route Overlap Chart
body: A leaked network map shows the post-merger plan consists mostly of amputations with arrows.

title: Gate Covenant Trap
body: A monetized gate package turns out to contain just enough restrictions to feel like a trap in a tie.

title: Seniority Map Fight
body: Workers discover that your elegant integration plan starts by erasing someone else's future.

title: Scope Clause Arbitration
body: The shell-game org chart arrives in front of an arbitrator with a very sharp pencil.

title: Covenant Default Clock
body: The lenders produce a timetable so precise it feels like a hostage video with spreadsheets.

title: Lender Cooperation Cracks
body: The friendliest creditors stop taking your calls and start comparing notes with each other instead.

title: Vendor Lien Blitz
body: Suppliers discover religion, paperwork, and the exact parts of your operation they can legally choke.

title: Inspection Memo Leak
body: An internal memo about deferred work escapes into daylight and immediately finds a microphone.

title: Parts Cannibalization
body: The fleet starts donating organs to itself, which is efficient right up until it becomes public.

title: Shell Route Map Leak
body: A leaked route matrix makes the shell carrier look less like strategy and more like labor evasion with branding.

title: Nominee Structure Leak
body: An ownership diagram escapes the vault and reveals just how many layers of deniability you bought.

title: Fairness Opinion Under Fire
body: The fairness opinion now requires its own fairness opinion.

title: Turnaround Story Cracks
body: The market finally notices that the comeback story is mostly typography and selective arithmetic.

title: Director Indemnity Alarm
body: The board becomes fascinated by one topic only: whether you have quietly protected yourself better than them.

title: Customs Broker Ping
body: A routine offshore transfer attracts exactly the kind of curious intermediary you pay other people to notice first.
